behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between config-file load, env override collection, and validation/error formatting. A unit test of only the validator could miss wiring bugs (e.g., missing env-to-key mapping before validation). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only local config/env processing with no subprocess/network/LLM.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader with a temp config file missing `database.host` and no corresponding `TOOL_...` env var; assert startup fails and message contains exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is path-resolution logic driven by environment input; minimum failure boundary is a single resolver function/object. Rejected Integration: behavior text focuses on chosen path computation, not cross-module state propagation. Rejected higher levels: no real subprocess/network/LLM requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Call path-resolution logic with `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/custom` and a known home directory; assert resolved path is `/tmp/custom/tool/config.yaml` (not `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`).

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundaries: YAML file read -> env var decoding/prefix mapping -> merge precedence. A pure unit test of merge logic could miss env decoding/key-mapping defects. Rejected higher levels: no subprocess/network/LLM in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use a temp YAML file with `database.host: filehost`, set matching `TOOL_...` env var to `envhost`, run loader end-to-end in-process, assert effective config uses `envhost`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Must exercise real YAML parser + loader error translation together; mocking parser errors can hide real exception-shape/line-field mismatches. Rejected System: no subprocess/real network/OS-only behavior required beyond local file read. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI step.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML to a temp config file (known bad line), run loader, assert failure message includes the parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Needs module wiring plus real local filesystem side effects (mkdir + file write + subsequent proceed path). This matches Integration (local filesystem) and catches ordering/state bugs. Rejected System: behavior text does not require subprocess, distributed network, or OS event semantics beyond ordinary local FS ops.
behaviors.test_description: Start with an empty temp home/config root where target config directory is absent, run loader startup, assert directory is created, defaults file exists with expected baseline content, and loader continues without fatal error.
